title: Binary Tree Center
description:
The center of a tree is the node(s) minimizing the maximum distance to any other node. It lies at the middle of a diameter path:
- If the diameter has an ODD number of nodes → 1 center
- If the diameter has an EVEN number of nodes → 2 centers

Given a tree in level-order, output the value(s) of the center node(s).
inputSpec: A single line: level-order encoding (integers and 'N').
outputSpec: If 1 center: 'Single X'. If 2 centers: 'Double X Y'.

botCodes.code:
import sys
sys.setrecursionlimit(20000)
vals=input().split()
n=len(vals)
left=[-1]*n;right=[-1]*n
for i in range(n):
    l,r=2*i+1,2*i+2
    if l<n and vals[l]!='N':left[i]=l
    if r<n and vals[r]!='N':right[i]=r

def diam(i):
    if i<0 or vals[i]=='N':return 0,[],0,[]
    hl,hpl,dl,dpl=diam(left[i])
    hr,hpr,dr,dpr=diam(right[i])
    h,hp=(hl+1,[vals[i]]+hpl) if hl>=hr else (hr+1,[vals[i]]+hpr)
    cand=hl+hr+1
    lp=list(reversed(hpl)) if left[i]>=0 else []
    rp=hpr if right[i]>=0 else []
    cp=lp+[vals[i]]+rp
    if cand>=dl and cand>=dr:bd,bp=cand,cp
    elif dl>=dr:bd,bp=dl,dpl
    else:bd,bp=dr,dpr
    return h,hp,bd,bp

_,_,d,dp=diam(0)
mid=(d-1)//2
if d%2==1:print('Single',dp[mid])
else:print('Double',dp[mid],dp[mid+1])
